items.title: Malaysia hosts IAEA’s only Asia‑Pacific nuclear security equipment hub, bolstering global preparedness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Malaysia is increasingly playing a vital role in bolstering global nuclear security and emergency preparedness through practical international cooperation. The repository at the Malaysia Nuclear Security Support Centre (NSSC) in Dengkil, Bangi, also serves as a regional hub for training and capacity-building, underscoring the country’s growing contribution to international nuclear security cooperation and risk management. IAEA Division of Nuclear Security Director Elena Buglova said the repository forms part of the agency’s broader global nuclear security network by improving operational support and regional preparedness for nuclear security incidents.

items.title: GDP surprise fuels hawkish OPR bets, lifts bond yields
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Investors have turned more hawkish on Malaysia's interest rate outlook after the economy grew faster than expected in the second quarter, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd. However, it expects Bank Negara Malaysia to leave the OPR unchanged at 2.75 per cent for the foreseeable future, saying the stronger growth has yet to translate into broad-based inflationary pressures. The figure beat CIMB Securities' forecast of 4.8 per cent and the market consensus of 5.2 per cent.

items.title: PPR owners subletting units undermine housing scheme's purpose, says Johari
items.summary: Titiwangsa member of parliament Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said Kuala Lumpur City Hall's (DBKL) decision to halt the sale of PPR units was appropriate following cases of misuse by some owners. Johari said this after attending a breakfast and meet-the-people session with residents of Perkasa PPR here yesterday. In addition, Johari suggested DBKL conduct block-by-block surveys among PPR residents to obtain majority consent before deciding on the sale of units.

items.title: Kelantan police station chief arrested over RM500 bribe from UNHCR card holder
items.summary: The 41-year-old suspect, who was serving as the officer in charge of a police station (OCS), was arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) about 2.20pm yesterday. Kelantan MACC director Azmin Yusuff confirmed that the suspect was arrested under Section 17(a) of the MACC Act 2009. Earlier, the suspect, clad in the MACC's orange lock-up attire, arrived at the court about 9.05am, escorted by two MACC officers.

items.title: Four remaining MV Berkat Tuah crew members return home safely
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The four remaining Malaysian crew members of the MV Berkat Tuah, who were stranded at Hamriyah Port in Sharjah, United Arab Emirates, returned home safely today. In a statement, the Foreign Ministry said the crew members arrived at Kuala Lumpur International Airport at 9am. Four other Malaysian crew members from the same vessel had earlier returned to Malaysia on April 10.

items.title: UniSZA pulls down 50 clinic banners after controversial ads spark backlash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Universiti Sultan Zainal Abidin (UniSZA) has removed 50 promotional banners for Klinik Perubatan UMC after the advertisements went viral and sparked controversy on social media. The university said UniSZA Holdings Sdn Bhd (UHSB) was instructed to remove all the promotional materials within less than five hours of installation to prevent misunderstanding and discomfort among the public. The operations of Klinik Perubatan UMC, including the management of promotional materials, fall under UniSZA Medical Services Sdn Bhd, a subsidiary of UHSB.

items.title: MCMC orders telcos to act after Tawau village coverage fails mandatory standards
items.summary: TAWAU, July 20 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has directed telecommunications service providers to take immediate measures to improve the quality of communications coverage in Kampung Tinagat here. In a statement, MCMC said the directive followed a working visit and assessment of communications coverage and service performance conducted at three identified locations in the village. MCMC said Wireless Broadband Access Service (WBAS) tests found that two of the three locations assessed failed to meet the commission’s mandatory standards.

items.title: China leaves benchmark lending rates unchanged for 14th consecutive month in July
items.summary: SHANGHAI: China kept benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 14th consecutive month on Monday, in line with market expectations. The unchanged loan prime rates (LPRs) suggest that policymakers remain patient, keeping interest rates on hold despite softer-than-expected second-quarter economic data that underscored the uneven growth in the world's second-largest economy. China's economy is facing a structural mismatch between strong supply and weak demand, the People's Bank of China (PBOC) said earlier this month, pledging to maintain an appropriately loose monetary policy and ramp up financial support to revive domestic consumption.

items.title: China securities regulator to hold market stability meeting, state media reports
items.summary: SHANGHAI/BEIJING: China's securities watchdog will meet market participants on Monday to discuss market stability, official media said, as state-backed investors bought shares in an effort to stem a rapid slide in prices. The move follows a rout that wiped out 10 trillion yuan (US$1.48 trillion) worth of China market capitalisation over the past two weeks. Two state-owned firms said over the weekend they had deployed roughly 60 billion yuan to buy stocks.

items.title: Foreign funds extend buying streak, net inflows jump eightfold to RM612.3mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Foreign institutions extended two consecutive weeks of net buying, recording RM612.3 million in net inflows, around eight times higher than the previous week's inflows of RM75.3 million, according to MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB IB). MBSB IB said the top three sectors that recorded net inflows from foreign institutions were financial services (RM754.1 million), utilities (RM194.9 million), and plantation (RM66.9 million). The largest net outflows were recorded in industrial products and services (-RM256.0 million), technology (-RM98.5 million), and consumer products and services (-RM64.3 million).

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain warnings issued for several states including Selangor, Penang and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning covering parts of 10 states and Putrajaya, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until early afternoon today. On its Facebook page, MetMalaysia said the warning, issued at 8.50am, involved areas in Kedah, Penang, Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Putrajaya, Negeri Sembilan and Sarawak. The affected areas include Kuala Muda in Kedah; Penang; Machang, Pasir Puteh, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang in Kelantan; as well as Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus and Hulu Terengganu in Terengganu.

items.title: Remains found in France belong to woman missing since 2020: Prosecutor
items.summary: PARIS: Human remains found in France last week belong to a woman missing since 2020, a prosecutor said on Sunday, after her husband confessed to killing her and hiding her body. In a case that has gripped France since the height of the Covid-19 pandemic, Cedric Jubillar, 38, was sentenced to 30 years in prison in October for murdering Delphine Jubillar, 33. Her body had never been found and Jubillar had always maintained his innocence, including during the trial.

items.title: World Cup final crowd leaves Dataran Merdeka strewn with rubbish
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has expressed disappointment over the amount of rubbish left behind at Dataran Merdeka after thousands gathered to watch the FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina early yesterday. In a Facebook post, DBKL said that while the gathering reflected the public's passion for football, the historic square was left strewn with litter following the celebrations. "Kuala Lumpur is our home.

items.title: DBKL seizes equipment, issues 19 compounds against unlicensed photographers in KLCC area
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) issued 19 compound notices and seized equipment from individuals allegedly operating as unlicensed photographers during an enforcement operation around the KLCC area on Saturday night. The operation targeted unauthorised photography services, commonly referred to as “ulat foto”, being offered at public spaces, DBKL said in a statement shared on its Facebook page. The local authority said it issued 19 compound notices to Malaysians found operating photography services without permission and seized 28 items of equipment, including mobile phones, with seven of the seizures involving foreign nationals.

items.title: We'll lose in Negri if Malay vote is split, KJ says on BN-PN tie-up
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan state election could lead to a landslide victory for both coalitions if Malay votes are not split, says former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin. Acknowledging dissatisfaction among several division leaders in Negri Sembilan over seat allocations to Pas and Wawasan, Khairy warned that a split in the Malay vote would inevitably result in defeat. While acknowledging the anxiety among Umno members over the BN-PN partnership in Negri Sembilan, he said the state's political dynamics differed from those in Johor.

items.title: Op Tiris 4.0: Ministry foils 12 attempts to siphon subsidised fuel worth over RM800,000
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has foiled 12 attempts to siphon subsidised fuel and detained 27 people under Op Tiris 4.0 since the operation began in March. Kuala Lumpur ministry chief enforcement officer Mohd Shahran Mohd Arshad said the 12 cases involved seizures worth more than RM800,000. "Since the operation began until today, the ministry's Kuala Lumpur branch has recorded 12 cases under Op Tiris 4.0.

items.title: ‘Celebrate, don’t trash’: DBKL slams World Cup final fans over Dataran Merdeka litter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has reminded the public to take responsibility for keeping shared spaces clean after Dataran Merdeka was left strewn with rubbish following a World Cup 2026 final viewing event. In a Facebook post today, DBKL said it was saddened by the condition of the historic site after thousands of football fans gathered there early Monday morning to watch the final between Spain and Argentina. “Early this morning, thousands gathered to cheer, celebrate football and share the excitement.

items.title: Israel PM's office slams NY mayor over Netanyahu arrest remarks
items.summary: TEL AVIV: The office of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday denounced New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani after he said he considering whether to arrest the Israeli leader during an upcoming UN summit. "Mr Mamdani should focus on fixing the damage his policies have caused New York," Netanyahu's office said, in a post on its official X account. "I believe that Prime Minister Netanyahu belongs in The Hague," Mamdani told The New York Times.

items.title: HLIB: PetDag fuel supply remains stable despite Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Dagangan Bhd (PetDag) continues to maintain stable fuel supply and inventory levels despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, according to Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB). HLIB said PetDag's integrated inventory management system, supported by automatic tank gauging technology and close coordination with Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), has ensured uninterrupted fuel availability at petrol stations nationwide. However, HLIB expects PetDag's retail segment performance to moderate in the second quarter (Q2) of financial year 2026 (FY26) following a sharp decline in crude oil prices.

items.title: Court acquits Zizie Izette of three charges linked to RM2.8m bribery case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Actress Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad was acquitted today of three charges of abetting in a corruption case involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts. Zizie Izette was accused of abetting Bung Moktar, who was then Felcra Berhad’s non-executive chairman, in receiving RM2.2 million in cash bribes from Public Mutual Berhad investment agent Madhi Abdul Hamid. The third charge involved an alleged RM337,500 received from another Public Mutual Berhad investment agent, Norhaili Ahmad Mokhtar, through a Public Ittikal Sequel Fund (PITSEQ) account registered under her name.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia opens higher on expected sector rotation
items.summary: Among heavyweight stocks, Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) fell four sen to RM11.16, Public Bank Bhd declined three sen to RM5.22, while CIMB Group Holdings Bhd and IHH Healthcare Bhd were unchanged at RM7.82 and RM8.50, respectively. Tenaga Nasional Bhd gained eight sen to RM14.38. Among actively traded stocks, VS Industry Bhd shed half a sen to 22.5 sen, while Pegasus Heights Bhd, Ingenieur Gudang Bhd, PUC Bhd, Tanco Holdings Bhd and Aimax Bhd were unchanged at half a sen, four sen, four sen, 33.5 sen and one sen, respectively.

items.title: Khairy: Malay vote unity could help BN, Perikatan reclaim Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said Selangor could be won back if Malay votes were no longer split, pointing to possible cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) as a potential formula. Khairy said the cooperation between BN and PN would also serve as a test of whether the consolidation of Malay votes could benefit BN, PAS and Bersatu. The former Rembau MP also said there appeared to be issues with BN’s current cooperation with parties in the unity government.

items.title: Hungary's PM Magyar to ask chess champion Judit Polgar to become president
items.summary: BUDAPEST: Prime Minister Peter Magyar said on Sunday that he would ask Judit Polgar, widely regarded as the greatest female chess player of all time, to take up the largely ceremonial role of Hungary's next President. Magyar said in a Facebook post that Polgar, 49, could represent the nation's unity, and that on Monday he would meet Polgar and ask her if she would accept the nomination. Judit Polgar and her two sisters - Zsuzsa (Susan) and Zsofia (Sofia) - along with Ildiko Madl won the 1988 Chess Olympiad for Hungary's female team, ending Soviet dominance.

items.title: Gold slips as oil prices advance, Fed rate hike voices grow
items.summary: BENGALURU: Gold prices fell on Monday as an escalation in the Middle East conflict pushed Brent crude above US$90 a barrel, heightening inflation concerns as several US Federal Reserve policymakers signalled that interest rate hikes may be needed to curb price pressures. US gold futures for August delivery eased 0.3 per cent to US$4,005.90. * Oil prices jumped three per cent, with Brent surpassing US$90 a barrel.

items.title: Zizie Izette freed of abetting late husband Bung Moktar in RM2.8mil corruption case [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Zizie Izette A. Samad was acquitted by the Sessions Court today of three charges of abetting her late husband in obtaining RM2.8 million in bribes linked to Felcra Bhd's RM150 million unit trust investment. Judge Rosli Ahmad ruled that the prosecution had failed to prove its case beyond a reasonable doubt, freeing the 48-year-old former actress-turned-businesswoman, who is the widow of the late Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin. Zizie Izette broke down in tears after the verdict was delivered, while several members of her family watched from the public gallery.

items.title: From qualifiers to hosts? Zahid says Malaysia aspires to stage Fifa World Cup
items.summary: BANGI, July 20 — Malaysia aspires to host the Fifa World Cup, the world’s biggest football tournament, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. “God willing, one day we aspire to make Malaysia a host of the World Cup. The Harimau Malaya (national football squad) have climbed to 136th in the latest Fifa world rankings.

items.title: Police confirm sudden death of 12‑year‑old footballer during U‑12 tournament in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 20 — A 12-year-old boy, Danish Hizqeel Mohd Hanif, died after collapsing while taking part in an Under-12 (U-12) football tournament at a sports centre here Saturday. “His passing is a tremendous loss to the entire Shining Academy family. Meanwhile, the Johor Bahru Football Association (JBFA) said Danish had shown great potential in football and that his passing was deeply felt by the state’s football community.

items.title: Economists see inflation manageable despite rising cost pressures, no OPR cut needed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's inflation is expected to remain manageable this year despite mounting upside risks from higher global oil prices, electricity tariffs, weather-related food supply disruptions and volatile grain prices, economists said. Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) projected Malaysia's consumer price index to rise 2.1 per cent in 2026, while Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) maintained its forecast at 2.0 per cent. Malaysia's headline inflation eased to 1.9 per cent in June from 2.0 per cent in May, while core inflation also moderated to 1.9 per cent from 2.0 per cent.

items.title: RTM set to unveil more major sports broadcasts after World Cup coverage success, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) will announce the acquisition of broadcasting rights for several more world-class sporting events following the successful free-to-air coverage of the entire 2026 Fifa World Cup, said Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil. “Broadcasting director-general Ashwad Ismail has informed me that RTM would be airing several more tournaments. That will be announced later,” he told reporters after attending the Unity Merdeka x RTM World Cup 2026 programme at Dataran Merdeka early this morning.

items.title: N. Sembilan polls: Anthony Loke urges campaign on what parties offer, not who they attack
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 20 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said election campaigns should focus on the policies and offerings that parties intend to bring to the people, rather than using personal attacks. That was why he has chosen not to launch personal attacks against any Cabinet member throughout the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election campaign period, he added. I have taken the position of not attacking any leader or bringing up anything, especially involving Cabinet ministers, because they are my colleagues in the Cabinet.

items.title: Asia shares hesitant as oil climbs, earnings loom
items.summary: Futures imply a 60 per cent chance of a rate rise as early as September, pushing yields on 30 year Treasuries back above the psychological 5.0 per cent barrier. HIGH EXPECTATIONS FOR EARNINGS BofA analyst Savita Subramanian remains upbeat on the earnings outlook, forecasting a 5 per cent beat versus consensus, or 28 per cent growth. Such forecasts helped S&P 500 futures edge up 0.2 per cent in early trading, while Nasdaq futures firmed 0.4 per cent.

items.title: Ipoh retiree lured by Telegram group, loses RM150,000 in bogus sports company scam
items.summary: Kampar police chief Supt Mohamad Nazri Daud said the victim, a 67-year-old retiree, lodged a police report yesterday after realising he had been cheated. He said investigations found the complainant clicked a link about the investment on social media and was added to a Telegram group at the end of last September. Mohamad Nazri said when the complainant wanted to withdraw his capital and profits early this month, the suspect instructed him to make additional payments totalling RM20,000.

items.title: In China, people brave simulated typhoons to prepare for the real thing
items.summary: HANGZHOU, (China): Huang Zhen, 34, shut his cafe for a day last week to be buffeted by surging waters and powerful winds that knocked him off his feet — training for extreme weather events that are increasingly afflicting China. Huang was one of a dozen people attending a session at the Lvxing Disaster Simulation Training Center in Hangzhou, eastern China. "Over the last two years, the weather has become increasingly unpredictable," Huang said.

items.title: Zizie Izette arrives in KL court to hear verdict in RM2.8m corruption abetment case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad arrived at the Kuala Lumpur Court Complex today to hear the verdict on whether she will be acquitted or convicted on three charges of abetting in a corruption case involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts. Sessions Court Judge Rosli Ahmad is scheduled to deliver the verdict at 9am at the end of the defence case. Zizie Izette, 47, the wife of the late Kinabatangan Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, is facing three charges of abetting her husband in receiving cash bribes totalling RM2.8 million.

items.title: Brent oil tops US$90 as US, Iran intensify attacks in Middle East
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Oil prices jumped three per cent on Monday, with Brent surpassing US$90 a barrel, as the United States and Iran expanded attacks in the Middle East that have curbed energy shipments in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude futures climbed US$2.69, or 3.05 per cent, to US$90.79 by 2343 GMT, touching the highest level since June 11 and extending gains after rising 15.9 per cent last week, its biggest weekly gain since April. US West Texas Intermediate crude was at US$84.68 a barrel, up US$2.19, or 2.65 per cent, the highest since June 12.

items.title: Pakatan sets 23‑seat target as Anthony Loke rallies for stronger Negeri Sembilan mandate
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 20 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has set a target of winning at least 23 of the 36 seats in the Negeri Sembilan state election on August 1 to secure a stronger and more stable mandate to govern the state, said its deputy chairman Anthony Loke. Loke, who also DAP secretary-general, said the coalition currently had 17 assemblymen and would require at least 19 seats to secure a simple majority, but stressed that a larger mandate was necessary to ensure a stable administration. “Currently we have 17 seats.

items.title: DBKL launches probe with arborists after fallen tree kills man on Jalan Tun Razak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is conducting a detailed technical investigation to determine the cause of a fallen tree incident on Jalan Tun Razak that claimed the life of a man on Saturday night. DBKL said the investigation is jointly carried out with the Malaysian Society of Arborist, covering an assessment of the tree’s physical condition, root structure, soil condition, environmental factors, as well as records of inspection, maintenance and previous complaints related to the tree and its location. Initial inspections involve trees along Jalan Ampang, Jalan Sultan Ismail, Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Datuk Keramat, as well as parts of Jalan Tun Razak.

items.title: Ringgit opens higher against US dollar, major currencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar and major currencies on Monday, supported by stronger-than-expected advance estimates showing Malaysia's economy expanded faster in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2 2026). At 8am, the local currency strengthened to 4.0920/0995 against the greenback from Friday's close of 4.0930/0990. He said the US Dollar Index (DXY) remained well supported, rising 0.08 per cent from the previous close to 100.846 points.

items.title: Zizie Izette arrives at court to hear verdict on RM2.8 million corruption abetment case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datin Seri Zizie Izette Abdul Samad arrived at the Kuala Lumpur Court Complex today to hear the verdict on whether she will be acquitted or convicted on three charges of abetting in a corruption case involving RM2.8 million linked to a RM150 million investment in Public Mutual Berhad unit trusts. Zizie Izette, 47, the wife of the late Kinabatangan Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Bung Moktar Radin, arrived at the court at 8.30am. On Nov 18, 2024, the Court of Appeal allowed the prosecution's appeal against the Sept 7, 2023, High Court's decision, acquitting the couple.

items.title: Two Terengganu teenagers killed, another critically hurt in three-motorcycle crash in Kuala Nerus
items.summary: He said the SYM BX110 rider, Muhamad Hazim Mohd Zawawi, 17, a restaurant assistant, was travelling from Kampung Pecah Rotan to his home in Kampung Mengabang Kapal. The Yamaha Ego Avantiz rider, Muhammad Rifqi Rayyan Abdul Rahim, 14, a student at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Batu Rakit, was travelling from Batu Rakit towards Kampung Pecah Rotan. The third rider, Muhammad Harris Iman Fadillah, 15, was riding a Honda EX5 from Kampung Pecah Rotan to his home in Kampung Kubur Timah.

items.title: Methane leak at Miri worksite confirmed, monitoring continues
items.summary: MIRI: Methane gas detected leaking from a bore pile at a construction site along Jalan Perdana, Tudan yesterday has been confirmed as natural methane gas, prompting continued monitoring and a detailed technical assessment by multiple safety agencies. Upon arrival, the team found no fire or explosion but began monitoring the surrounding area to ensure the construction site remained safe. "A Petros team arrived at 9.40am to identify the gas leaking from the bore pile.

items.title: Dollar firmer as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent hits US$90
items.summary: The US dollar climbed 0.1 per cent to 162.48 yen, the greenback's strongest level since July 9, as geopolitical tensions rose and investors sought safe haven assets. The euro was down 0.1 per cent at US$1.1426, while the British pound was unchanged at US$1.3445. The Australian dollar slipped 0.1 per cent to US$0.6975, while the New Zealand dollar declined 0.2 per cent to US$0.5833.

items.title: Samsung Biologics to launch US$1.8 billion all cash bid for PolyPeptide
items.summary: BENGALURU: South Korea's Samsung Biologics will launch a 1.46 billion Swiss franc (US$1.81 billion) all cash bid for Swiss contract drugmaker PolyPeptide, the companies said in separate statements. The tender offer is expected to launch by the end of August and close by year end, Samsung Biologics said. PolyPeptide said its board had unanimously recommended shareholders accept the offer.

items.title: Methane gas leak halts work at Miri construction site after explosive-level reading
items.summary: MIRI, July 20 — A methane gas leak detected from a piling hole at a construction site along Jalan Perdana in Tudan has prompted a multi-agency safety operation after tests confirmed the gas reached 100 per cent of its Lower Explosive Limit (LEL) at the source. According to Lutong Fire and Rescue Station chief Henry Jugah, firefighters were alerted at 7.45am and dispatched to the site to conduct initial monitoring and gas readings after reports of gas escaping from the piling hole, Berita Harian reported. Initial Hazmat readings taken at 6.45pm again recorded 100 per cent LEL at the piling hole, while gas concentrations of 32.5 per cent LEL were detected one metre from the source.

items.title: #NSTviral: Netizens react after gym-goer highlights matcha latte 'theft', confronts culprit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A man's account of a stranger taking his drink at a gym has gone viral on Threads , drawing thousands of reactions after he shared CCTV footage and a play-by-play of the confrontation that followed. When he returned, the drink had vanished. The video revealed another man taking the drink and bringing it over to a treadmill.

items.title: E-invoicing curbs leakages, boosts national revenue
items.summary: Data analytics from e-invoices allow IRB to identify individuals or businesses with active financial transactions, including high-value purchases, who may have unreported income. Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican said the implementation of e-invoicing since August 2024 has seen more than 52,540 taxpayers voluntarily declare RM4.07 billion in additional taxable income, yielding RM1.009 billion in taxes due. He said e-invoicing was not intended to burden traders or impose additional taxes, but to provide the government with transaction data for analysis and tax compliance.

items.title: Two teens killed, another in ICU after three-motorcycle crash
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: Two teenage motorcyclists were killed and another was severely injured in a three-motorcycle collision at Batu Rakit, Kuala Nerus on Sunday. Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the deceased, aged 14 and 17, died at the scene. They were riding a Yamaha Ego and a SYM BX110 respectively.

items.title: Dataran Merdeka turns into roaring stadium as 25,000 fans cheer Argentina vs Spain showdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Dataran Merdeka, the historic landmark in the capital, transformed into an ‘open-air stadium’ as more than 25,000 football fans gathered to watch the live broadcast of the 2026 Fifa World Cup final early this morning. The title showdown between Argentina and Spain was broadcast on a giant screen by Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) as part of the Unity Merdeka x RTM World Cup 2026 programme. The title showdown between Argentina and Spain was broadcast on a giant screen by Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) as part of the Unity Merdeka x RTM World Cup 2026 programme.

items.title: Airbus sees strong potential for A220 in Southeast Asia
items.summary: Airbus Asia Pacific president Anand Stanley said the aircraft is suited for markets where geography, distance and route size made smaller narrow-body aircraft relevant. Stanley said the A220 programme had crossed 1,100 aircraft orders globally, while Airbus was also seeing strong order momentum from Asia Pacific. Stanley said Airbus is looking at demand for almost 20,000 new aircraft in Asia Pacific, with 16,000 of them single-aisle aircraft.

items.title: Six Negeri Sembilan seats that could tell the story of the election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Six seats could tell the story of the Negeri Sembilan election long before the final results are in. If there is one seat synonymous with Umno in Negeri Sembilan, it is Rantau. Rantau is more diverse than many may expect, with Malays making up 54.8 per cent of voters, Indians 27.6 per cent and Chinese 16 per cent.

items.title: Perlis Education Dept vows firm action after cops detain three Kangar schoolboys suspected of bullying Form One classmate
items.summary: KANGAR, July 19 — The Perlis Education Department (JPN) will not compromise on any form of bullying in educational institutions, and firm action will be taken against anyone found guilty. In a statement today, the department said it is taking seriously an incident allegedly involving student misconduct that resulted in injuries at a school in Perlis. Yesterday, it was reported that police had detained three Form One male students from a secondary school here on suspicion of bullying and causing injuries to a male student of the same age on Thursday.

items.title: Pakatan to reveal its game plan for Negeri Sembilan polls with new manifesto Monday night
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) will unveil its manifesto for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election tomorrow night. Negeri Sembilan PH Operations director Datuk Muhammad Zaki Md Sabri said the manifesto will focus on sustaining development and prosperity enjoyed by the people since PH took over the state government in 2018. “Insya-Allah, the manifesto will be launched tomorrow night.

items.title: Terengganu pulls down viral banners after ‘inappropriate’ wording sparks complaints
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 19 — Advertising banners displaying obscene wording in the city, which went viral on social media yesterday, have been taken down. Terengganu Local Government, Housing and Health Committee chairman Datuk Wan Sukairi Wan Abdullah said checks found that the permit for the banner installation had been approved based on the materials submitted during the application, but the content had been altered before they were displayed at public locations in Kuala Terengganu City. He said the Kuala Terengganu City Council (MBKT) subsequently contacted the relevant parties and instructed that the advertising banners be removed immediately.

items.title: Campaign freely, but don’t cross the line, Anwar tells ministers and govt leaders
items.summary: IPOH, July 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded individuals holding positions in the government to observe discipline and refrain from attacking the Federal Government while campaigning in the state elections. Anwar said the matter had been discussed with his two deputies, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. The Prime Minister, who is also Pakatan Harapan chairman, said parties within the Unity Government had also been urged to preserve cordial relations at both the federal and state levels to continue delivering effective services to the people.

items.title: Why must only BN quit govt over election alliances? Umno Youth chief asks Pakatan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 19 — Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Akmal Saleh has challenged Pakatan Harapan (PH) Youth to demand that Warisan and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) also leave the federal government if it believes Barisan Nasional (BN) ministers should resign over BN’s cooperation with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in state elections. He said PH Youth should also pressure Warisan to leave the federal government as the party had contested against PH in the previous Sabah state election while being part of the federal administration. “I ask PH Youth to also demand that Warisan leave because they also fought against PH in the previous Sabah state election, when they were part of the federal government,” he said.

items.title: From pushing motorbikes to stealing them: Police nab four teens after 20 motorcycle theft cases reported near Sg Petani KTMB station
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, July 19 — Police have arrested four teenage boys, including three brothers, suspected of stealing motorcycles around the Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) parking area near here on July 14. He said police had received reports of 20 motorcycle theft cases in the area. Hanyan said all four suspects had no previous criminal records, while the alleged gang leader, 15, was not attending school.

items.title: Forget big walkabouts — Aminuddin says he’d rather meet Linggi voters one-on-one
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 19 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he prefers a small-scale campaign approach to enable him to better understand the needs of the local community. Aminuddin, who is contesting the Linggi seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election under Pakatan Harapan (PH), said he has adopted this approach since 2008 to allow him to interact more effectively with voters, while making them feel more comfortable. In the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, Aminuddin is being challenged by Mohd Faizal, 54, and Zamri Md Said, 54, from Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu).

items.title: MP wants full probe after Penang police rescue Cameroonian woman in Bukit Mertajam human trafficking raid
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 19 — Bukit Mertajam MP Steven Sim has called on the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) to carry out a comprehensive investigation into the human trafficking syndicate uncovered in Berapit, Bukit Mertajam, to ensure that everyone involved, including the masterminds, is swiftly brought to justice. The Minister of Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development said the successful operation by the PDRM, particularly the Seberang Perai Tengah Police headquarters and Bukit Aman, reflected the force’s commitment and effectiveness in combating cross-border crime. Sim said the Bukit Mertajam MP’s Office would continue to closely monitor developments in the case and work with enforcement agencies, including the PDRM and the Immigration Department, to strengthen preventive measures.

items.title: Pakatan hopeful vows better roads, internet and jobs for Labu’s Orang Asli
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 19 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Labu state seat, Datuk Ahmad Faez Abdul Razak, is committed to empowering the economy and improving the living standards of the Orang Asli community in the area through infrastructure improvements and economic opportunities. “Education and youth development must also be prioritised as they have great potential but require support to enhance skills and generate income,” he told Bernama after attending the Orang Asli Women Empowerment programme at Kampung Orang Asli Tekir here today. Ahmad Faez said the Orang Asli community in the village has potential in handicraft production, which can be marketed more widely to boost community income.

items.title: Anwar hints at bigger STR, SARA payouts if economy stays on track
items.summary: IPOH, July 19 — The Madani Government is considering increasing assistance under the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) schemes if the country’s economic conditions permit, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Also present were Home Ministry deputy secretary-general (security) Datuk Abdul Halim Abdul Rahman and Rela director-general Datuk Rosli Yaakub. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said the government was also giving due attention to the welfare of security personnel, including Rela members, who help respond to the country’s security and disaster situations.
